notes:
Live codex-cli 0.144.5 /permissions dialog in its ALREADY-CURRENT state: the menu reads
"> 2. Approve for me (current)" — row 2 is BOTH the highlight and the "(current)" preset.
Companion to test/corpus/codex/permissions-dialog, which recorded the other starting state
("> 1. Ask for approval (current)"). Replayed through the shipped parser this yields
kind=permissions_prompt, three rows, row 2 highlighted, keys "2\\r"; readyForInput("codex") is
false and AutoDismissKeys returns [null,false], exactly as for the first fixture.

HOW TO REPRODUCE THIS STARTING STATE (probe recording: META-HARNESS-122).
Recorded by test/corpus/tools/probe-codex-permissions.ts --phase fixture --open-shape split,
under an ISOLATED CODEX_HOME seeded by test/helpers/codex_home.ts:
  * the home was seeded with a 0600 copy of ~/.codex/auth.json AND NOTHING ELSE — in
    particular it carried NO config.toml. A bare empty CODEX_HOME is not usable: codex lands
    on the first-run sign-in wall, which signinWallRE excludes from DetectInput and
    onboardingWall holds as not-ready, so readyForInput never fires. Omitting config.toml is
    what makes the run deterministic: codex renders its DEFAULTS, so the dialog opens on
    "> 1. Ask for approval (current)" every time. Copying a real config.toml would import
    whatever approvals_reviewer the developer already carries.
  * codex was then driven: open /permissions, commit row 2, clear the composer, re-open
    /permissions. The recording deliberately ENDS with the dialog on screen (no ESC backout,
    no /status box) because corpus fixtures replay bytes.raw into a 120x40 Screen and
    snapshot the FINAL frame.

EXTRA FIRST-RUN INTERSTITIAL (finding). A fresh CODEX_HOME renders a FOLDER-TRUST prompt
before the composer: "Do you trust the contents of this directory?" with "> 1. Yes, continue"
/ "2. No, quit" and the "Press enter to continue" footer (see screen-00-boot.txt). It is
already covered by the shipped ladder — codexBlockingInterstitial matches the footer and holds
not-ready, and a KindNotice auto-dismiss (bare CR) clears it, which this recording confirms
live. NOT rendered here but rendered on the SECOND launch of a home: the "Update available!"
notice, which appears once $CODEX_HOME/version.json advertises a newer release.

CONTAINMENT (probe 3). CODEX_HOME IS honoured for config.toml on 0.144.5. Committing
"Approve for me" wrote approvals_reviewer = "auto_review" into the ISOLATED
<CODEX_HOME>/config.toml (captured verbatim as isolated-config.toml alongside this file), and
the real ~/.codex/config.toml AND ~/.codex/auth.json were both sha256-identical before and
after the session — see the containment block below. Note the persisted marker is
approvals_reviewer = "auto_review"; no literal granular key is written to config.toml.

stdin.log records every keystroke escape-encoded and IS the byte pin for this session.
